SEC-ARCH-001.name: HTTPS Usage
SEC-ARCH-001.description: Site uses HTTPS for secure communication
SEC-ARCH-001.impact: Data transmitted in cleartext can be intercepted

SEC-HEAD-001.name: Strict-Transport-Security
SEC-HEAD-001.description: HSTS header is present and valid
SEC-HEAD-001.recommendation: Add 'Strict-Transport-Security' header with max-age set (e.g., max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains)

SEC-HEAD-002.name: Content-Security-Policy
SEC-HEAD-002.description: CSP header is present to prevent XSS
SEC-HEAD-002.impact: Site vulnerable to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks

SEC-HEAD-004.name: X-Content-Type-Options
SEC-HEAD-004.impact: Browser may execute non-executable files as scripts

SEC-HEAD-005.name: Referrer-Policy
SEC-HEAD-005.description: Controls referrer information sent to other sites
SEC-HEAD-005.impact: May leak sensitive information in URL to third parties

SEC-HEAD-006.name: Permissions-Policy
SEC-HEAD-006.description: Controls browser features available to the page
SEC-HEAD-006.impact: Unnecessary browser features enabled (e.g., camera, mic)

SEC-INP-001.name: External Links Safety
SEC-INP-001.recommendation: Add rel='noopener' or rel='noreferrer' to all external links (target='_blank')

SEC-INP-002.name: Mixed Content
SEC-INP-002.description: Page does not load insecure (HTTP) resources
SEC-INP-002.impact: Insecure resources compromise the security of the HTTPS page
SEC-INP-002.recommendation: Ensure all resources (scripts, images, styles) are loaded via HTTPS

SEC-DISC-001.name: Server Header
SEC-DISC-001.description: Server header does not leak detailed version info
SEC-DISC-001.impact: Reveulating server version helps attackers target specific vulnerabilities

SEC-DISC-002.name: X-Powered-By
SEC-DISC-002.impact: Reveals technology stack details to attackers
